title: The Both-And Wealth Embodiment
subtitle: A body-based practice for holding luxury and simplicity as a single, unified abundance
excerpt: A short embodiment practice for when the either-or belief around wealth has settled into the body. The practice uses a physical anchor (hand on heart) to name and hold both the simple and the exceptional as belonging equally to an abundant life.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 62 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796). The embodiment format draws on the release-and-allow structure from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel you have to choose between your current life and the wealthy life you are building, or when a simple pleasure makes you feel you are not 'on track' with wealth.

content.text: A short embodiment practice for the either-or belief: the sense that a wealthy life and your current ordinary life are on two different tracks. The practice names both as belonging simultaneously to an abundant life — and uses a physical anchor to make that naming felt rather than just stated.
Stand or sit comfortably. Place your left hand on your heart and your right hand on your belly. Take two slow breaths.
Picture something simple from today or yesterday that you enjoyed — a meal, a quiet hour, something small. Hold it in mind clearly.
Now picture something you would love to have more of — a luxury, a comfort, a specific experience that feels out of reach. Hold it alongside the first image. Both in mind at once.
Say this aloud, hands still in place:
I am rich in what I have. I am open to more. Both are true. Both are mine.
Say it three times. The words are simple; the hands on the body are what grounds the statement rather than just thinking it.
Take a final slow breath. Carry both images into the day (the simple and the aspirational) as equally part of the same abundant life.
Adapted from the Day 62 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The embodiment format draws on the body-anchor approach from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
